title: Mustardy German Pork Stew
description: A hearty and energizing German stew featuring pork, mustard, and peach juice, perfect for a cold winter's night, and Whole30-friendly.
instructions: In a large hot pot vessel, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium-high heat.
Add the pork cubes and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped onions and cook until they are translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the mustard, peach juice, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the chopped carrots and potatoes, and stir to combine.
Bring the stew to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes.
Knead the dough of your favorite Whole30-friendly bread to serve on the side.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
